description: Phase 4 — Execution Manager + Flow Workers: ExecutionRepository (DB access for executions + steps), ActionTreeWalker (pure tree-to-steps flattener with routing metadata), BullMQ queue factory, ExecutionManager (idempotency + pre-insert + root enqueue), worker infrastructure with exponential backoff and DLQ alerting, branch/enroll_sequence/emit_event action workers, and full wiring into index.ts.

userStories.title: ExecutionRepository — DB access for executions and steps
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a repository for `automation_executions` and `automation_execution_steps` tables so that the Execution Manager and action workers can persist and query execution state without duplicating DB logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/repositories/execution.repository.ts`
Use `const SCHEMA = 'platform_automation'`, `const EXECUTIONS_TABLE = \`${SCHEMA}.automation_executions\``, `const STEPS_TABLE = \`${SCHEMA}.automation_execution_steps\``

Export `Execution` interface: `{ id: string; rule_id: string; rule_version: number; action_tree_snapshot: unknown; event_id: string; event_type: string; entity_type: string | null; entity_id: string | null; status: string; started_at: Date | null; completed_at: Date | null }`

Export `Step` interface: `{ id: string; execution_id: string; action_type: string; action_params: unknown; output: unknown; status: string; attempt: number; error: string | null; started_at: Date | null; completed_at: Date | null }`

Export `InsertExecutionData` interface: `{ id: string; rule_id: string; rule_version: number; action_tree_snapshot: unknown; event_id: string; event_type: string; entity_type?: string | null; entity_id?: string | null; status?: string }`
Export `InsertStepData` interface: `{ id: string; execution_id: string; action_type: string; action_params?: unknown }`
Export `ExecutionRepository` class with `constructor(private readonly db: Knex)`
`insertExecution(data: InsertExecutionData): Promise<Execution>` — inserts with `status = data.status ?? 'running'` and `started_at = this.db.fn.now()`, returns inserted row
`findExecution(eventId: string, ruleId: string): Promise<Execution | null>` — SELECT WHERE `event_id = eventId AND rule_id = ruleId`, returns first row or null
`updateExecutionStatus(executionId: string, status: string, completedAt?: Date): Promise<void>` — UPDATE `status` and optionally `completed_at` WHERE `id = executionId`
`insertSteps(steps: InsertStepData[]): Promise<void>` — bulk insert into STEPS_TABLE with `status = 'pending'` and `attempt = 0`; if `steps.length === 0`, return immediately without touching DB

`updateStepStatus(stepId: string, status: string, extras?: { error?: string; output?: unknown; attempt?: number; startedAt?: Date; completedAt?: Date }): Promise<void>` — UPDATE relevant columns WHERE `id = stepId`; only set columns whose extras value is not undefined
`updateManyStepsStatus(stepIds: string[], status: string): Promise<void>` — UPDATE `status` WHERE `id IN (stepIds)`; if `stepIds.length === 0`, return immediately without touching DB
`findStepById(stepId: string): Promise<Step | null>` — SELECT WHERE `id = stepId`, returns row or null

`test/unit/execution.repository.test.ts` mocks Knex and covers: (1) `insertExecution` — verifies insert called with correct fields including status='running' and started_at; (2) `findExecution` — returns execution when found, returns null when not found; (3) `updateExecutionStatus` with completedAt — verifies both fields updated; (4) `insertSteps` with non-empty array — verifies bulk insert; (5) `insertSteps` with empty array — DB NOT touched; (6) `updateStepStatus` with all extras — verifies each column included; (7) `updateManyStepsStatus` with non-empty IDs — verifies whereIn + update; (8) `updateManyStepsStatus` with empty array — DB NOT touched; (9) `findStepById` — returns step when found, null when not found
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use Knex bulk insert pattern: `this.db(STEPS_TABLE).insert(rows)` — Knex accepts an array for bulk insert. For `updateStepStatus` extras, build the update object dynamically: only include a key if the extras value is not `undefined`. Do NOT use `.returning('*')` on bulk inserts unless you need the return value — `insertSteps` returns void so skip it. Follow the same Knex patterns already established in `RulesRepository`.

userStories.title: ActionNode types + ActionTreeWalker — pure tree flattener with routing metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function that walks the full action_tree recursively, assigns UUIDs to all nodes, encodes next-step and branch-routing metadata directly into each step's action_params, and returns a flat list of pre-insertable step records — so the Execution Manager can bulk-insert all steps before enqueuing a single BullMQ job.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-tree-walker.ts`
Export `RegularActionNode` type: `{ type: 'send_message' | 'send_email' | 'call_ai' | 'enroll_sequence' | 'emit_event' | 'call_webhook'; params: Record<string, unknown>; next?: ActionNode }`
Export `BranchActionNode` type: `{ type: 'branch'; condition: unknown; if_true: ActionNode; if_false: ActionNode }`
Export `ActionNode = RegularActionNode | BranchActionNode`
Export `StepRecord` interface: `{ id: string; execution_id: string; action_type: string; action_params: Record<string, unknown>; status: 'pending' }`
Export `walkActionTree(tree: ActionNode, executionId: string): StepRecord[]` — pure function, imports `randomUUID` from `node:crypto`

For a `RegularActionNode`: assign `id = randomUUID()`. `action_params = { ...node.params }`. If `node.next` exists, recursively walk `node.next` to get `nextSteps: StepRecord[]` and set `action_params._next_step_id = nextSteps[0].id`. Return `[currentStep, ...nextSteps]`.

For a `BranchActionNode`: assign `id = randomUUID()`. Walk `node.if_true` → `trueSteps`. Walk `node.if_false` → `falseSteps`. `_if_true_step_id = trueSteps[0].id`. `_if_false_step_id = falseSteps[0].id`. `_if_true_subtree_ids = trueSteps.map(s => s.id)`. `_if_false_subtree_ids = falseSteps.map(s => s.id)`. `action_params = { condition: node.condition, _if_true_step_id, _if_false_step_id, _if_true_subtree_ids, _if_false_subtree_ids }`. Return `[branchStep, ...trueSteps, ...falseSteps]`.
Root node is always `result[0]` — the first element of the returned array

`test/unit/action-tree-walker.test.ts` covers: (1) single regular node (no next): 1 step returned, correct action_type and params, no _next_step_id in action_params; (2) linear chain of 3 regular nodes: 3 steps, result[0]._next_step_id === result[1].id, result[1]._next_step_id === result[2].id, result[2] has no _next_step_id; (3) branch node with two leaf children: 3 steps total, result[0].action_type === 'branch', result[0].action_params._if_true_step_id === result[1].id, result[0].action_params._if_false_step_id === result[2].id, _if_true_subtree_ids = [result[1].id], _if_false_subtree_ids = [result[2].id]; (4) nested branch (outer branch where if_true is itself a branch with two leaves): 7 total steps, outer._if_true_subtree_ids has 3 IDs (inner branch + its two children), outer._if_false_subtree_ids has 1 ID; (5) all returned steps have status: 'pending' and execution_id matching the parameter; (6) all IDs are non-empty strings and globally unique across the result array
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The `_if_true_subtree_ids` and `_if_false_subtree_ids` arrays include the root step of each subtree — so `_if_true_step_id === _if_true_subtree_ids[0]`. This allows the branch worker to mark ALL steps in the losing subtree (including the losing root) as 'skipped' with a single `updateManyStepsStatus` call. `params` on RegularActionNode may be undefined in practice — use `node.params ?? {}` as the spread source. The function has no I/O — it's synchronous and pure except for `randomUUID` calls.

userStories.title: BullMQ Queue factory + ActionJobData type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed BullMQ Queue factory and a shared ActionJobData type so that the Execution Manager and all action workers share a consistent job data contract without duplicating the queue configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/queue/index.ts`
Import `Queue` from `bullmq`; import `type ExecutionContext` from `../services/field-interpolator.js`
Export `const QUEUE_NAME = 'automation-actions'`
Export `ActionJobData` interface: `{ execution_id: string; step_id: string; action_type: string; action_params: Record<string, unknown>; exec_ctx: ExecutionContext; event: Record<string, unknown> }`

Export `createQueue(connection: object): Queue<ActionJobData>` — returns `new Queue<ActionJobData>(QUEUE_NAME, { connection, defaultJobOptions: { attempts: 5, backoff: { type: 'custom' }, removeOnComplete: { count: 1000 }, removeOnFail: { count: 5000 } } })`
No unit tests required for this file (pure factory, no logic)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The `connection` parameter is typed as `object` to remain compatible with both a plain `{ host, port }` config object and an IORedis instance — BullMQ 5 accepts both. `attempts: 5` means 1 initial attempt + 4 retries. `backoff: { type: 'custom' }` tells BullMQ to use the `backoffStrategy` registered on the Worker (defined in the worker factory story). `removeOnComplete/removeOnFail` counts prevent Redis memory bloat over time.

userStories.title: ExecutionManager — idempotency, DB pre-insert, root job enqueue
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an ExecutionManager that replaces the no-op stub in index.ts, checks idempotency against the DB, pre-inserts all execution steps in one bulk call, and enqueues only the root BullMQ job — so the full action chain is persisted before any worker starts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/execution-manager.ts`

Import `randomUUID` from `node:crypto`; import `Queue` from `bullmq`; import `type ExecutionRepository` from `../repositories/execution.repository.js`; import `ActionJobData`, `QUEUE_NAME` from `../queue/index.js`; import `walkActionTree`, `type ActionNode` from `./action-tree-walker.js`; import `type MatchedRule` from `./rule-matcher.js`; import `type InboundEvent` from `../events/inbound-event.js`; import `type ExecutionManagerPort` from `./event-consumer.js`
Export `ExecutionManager` class that implements `ExecutionManagerPort`
Constructor: `constructor(private readonly repo: ExecutionRepository, private readonly queue: Queue<ActionJobData>)`
`async handle(rule: MatchedRule, event: InboundEvent): Promise<void>` implementation:
Step 1 — Idempotency: `const existing = await this.repo.findExecution(event.event_id, rule.rule.rule_id)`. If `existing` is non-null, return immediately.

Step 2 — Insert execution: `const execution = await this.repo.insertExecution({ id: randomUUID(), rule_id: rule.rule.rule_id, rule_version: rule.rule.rule_version, action_tree_snapshot: rule.rule.action_tree, event_id: event.event_id, event_type: event.event_type, entity_type: event.entity_type ?? null, entity_id: event.entity_id ?? null })`

Step 3 — Walk tree and pre-insert all steps: `const steps = walkActionTree(rule.rule.action_tree as ActionNode, execution.id)`. If `steps.length === 0`, call `this.repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution.id, 'completed', new Date())` and return.
Step 4 — Bulk insert: `await this.repo.insertSteps(steps)`

Step 5 — Enqueue root job: `const rootStep = steps[0]`. Call `await this.queue.add(rootStep.action_type, { execution_id: execution.id, step_id: rootStep.id, action_type: rootStep.action_type, action_params: rootStep.action_params, exec_ctx: rule.execCtx, event: event as Record<string, unknown> })`
`test/unit/execution-manager.test.ts`: mock `ExecutionRepository`, `Queue` (mock `queue.add` as `vi.fn()`), and `walkActionTree` from `./action-tree-walker.js` using `vi.mock`
Test: idempotency — `repo.findExecution` returns an existing execution object → `repo.insertExecution` NOT called, `queue.add` NOT called

Test: happy path — `repo.findExecution` returns null → `repo.insertExecution` called with correct fields; `walkActionTree` called with `rule.rule.action_tree` and the inserted execution.id; `repo.insertSteps` called with walker output; `queue.add` called with `(rootStep.action_type, { execution_id: execution.id, step_id: rootStep.id, action_type: rootStep.action_type, action_params: rootStep.action_params, exec_ctx: rule.execCtx, event })`
Test: empty tree — `walkActionTree` returns [] → `repo.insertSteps` called with [], `repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution.id, 'completed')` called, `queue.add` NOT called
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The `ExecutionManager` does NOT update `execution.status` to 'running' explicitly — `insertExecution` defaults status to 'running'. It also does NOT mark it 'completed' at this stage — that is the responsibility of the last action worker in the chain. The only exception is the empty-tree guard (step 3), which immediately completes the execution. `QUEUE_NAME` is imported but not used directly in `queue.add` — BullMQ's `Queue` instance already targets the named queue. `queue.add(jobName, data)` where jobName is the action_type string.

userStories.title: Worker factory + DLQ alert handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a worker factory that applies the correct exponential backoff retry configuration and registers a 'failed' event listener that emits a structured Datadog-compatible alert when a job exhausts all retries — so all Phase 4+ action workers get consistent fault handling without repeating the config.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/queue/worker-factory.ts`
Import `Worker`, `Job` from `bullmq`; import `type ActionJobData` from `./index.js`
Export `const RETRY_DELAYS: readonly number[] = [5000, 30000, 120000, 600000]` — custom backoff delays in ms: 5s, 30s, 2m, 10m
Export `createActionWorker(queueName: string, connection: object, processor: (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>, logger?: Pick<Console, 'error'>): Worker<ActionJobData>`

Creates `new Worker<ActionJobData>(queueName, processor, { connection, settings: { backoffStrategy: (attemptsMade: number) => RETRY_DELAYS[attemptsMade - 1] ?? RETRY_DELAYS[RETRY_DELAYS.length - 1] } })`
Registers `worker.on('failed', (job, err) => { ... })` handler on the created worker

Inside the 'failed' handler: if `!job`, return early. Check if this is a final failure: `job.attemptsMade >= (job.opts.attempts ?? 1)`. If final failure: emit structured alert via `(logger?.error ?? console.error)(JSON.stringify({ alert: 'automation_dlq', queue: queueName, execution_id: job.data.execution_id, step_id: job.data.step_id, action_type: job.data.action_type, error: err.message }))`.
Returns the worker

`test/unit/worker-factory.test.ts` mocks `Worker` from `bullmq` using `vi.mock('bullmq', () => ({ Worker: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({ on: vi.fn(), listenerCount: vi.fn() })) }))` — do NOT attempt to connect to real Redis
Test: `RETRY_DELAYS` is exactly `[5000, 30000, 120000, 600000]`
Test: `createActionWorker` calls `new Worker` with the correct `queueName` and `settings.backoffStrategy`

Test: `backoffStrategy(1)` returns 5000, `backoffStrategy(2)` returns 30000, `backoffStrategy(3)` returns 120000, `backoffStrategy(4)` returns 600000, `backoffStrategy(99)` returns 600000 (clamps to last value)

Test: 'failed' handler with final failure (`attemptsMade = 4`, `opts.attempts = 4`) — `logger.error` called with JSON string containing `alert: 'automation_dlq'`, `execution_id`, `step_id`, `action_type`, and `error`
Test: 'failed' handler with non-final failure (`attemptsMade = 1`, `opts.attempts = 4`) — `logger.error` NOT called
Test: 'failed' handler with `job = undefined` — no error thrown, `logger.error` NOT called
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: BullMQ's `backoffStrategy` in `settings` receives `attemptsMade` (number of attempts already made, starting at 1 for the first retry). So `RETRY_DELAYS[attemptsMade - 1]` maps attempt 1→5000ms, attempt 2→30000ms, etc. The `'failed'` event fires on EVERY failed attempt, not just the last. The final-failure check (`attemptsMade >= opts.attempts`) distinguishes DLQ-worthy failures from normal retries. The `Worker` mock in tests: capture the `on` calls by storing them in a map, then call them manually to test the handler. Alternatively, call the backoffStrategy function extracted from the `new Worker(...)` args directly.

userStories.title: branch worker — condition evaluation, winning path enqueue, losing path skip
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a branch action worker that evaluates the branch condition against the original event, marks all losing-path steps as skipped, loads and enqueues the winning-path's root step, and marks the branch step itself as completed — so conditional routing works correctly in the action chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-workers/branch.worker.ts`

Import `Job`, `Queue` from `bullmq`; import `type ActionJobData` from `../../queue/index.js`; import `type ExecutionRepository` from `../../repositories/execution.repository.js`; import `type { Condition }`, `evaluate` from `../condition-evaluator.js`
Export `createBranchProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`
The returned processor function:
1. Destructures `{ execution_id, step_id, action_params, exec_ctx, event }` from `job.data`
2. Calls `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`

3. Reads from action_params: `condition = action_params.condition as Condition`, `_if_true_step_id = action_params._if_true_step_id as string`, `_if_false_step_id = action_params._if_false_step_id as string`, `_if_true_subtree_ids = action_params._if_true_subtree_ids as string[]`, `_if_false_subtree_ids = action_params._if_false_subtree_ids as string[]`
4. Evaluates: `const result = evaluate(condition, event, exec_ctx)`
5. `winnerStepId = result ? _if_true_step_id : _if_false_step_id`; `losingIds = result ? _if_false_subtree_ids : _if_true_subtree_ids`
6. Calls `await repo.updateManyStepsStatus(losingIds, 'skipped')`
7. Calls `const winnerStep = await repo.findStepById(winnerStepId)`. If `winnerStep` is null, throws `new Error('Branch winner step not found: ' + winnerStepId)`

8. Calls `await queue.add(winnerStep.action_type, { execution_id, step_id: winnerStepId, action_type: winnerStep.action_type, action_params: winnerStep.action_params as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx, event })`
9. Calls `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date() })`
`test/unit/action-workers/branch-worker.test.ts` mocks `ExecutionRepository`, `Queue` (`queue.add` = `vi.fn()`), and `evaluate` from `../../services/condition-evaluator.js` (via `vi.mock`)
Test: condition true → `updateManyStepsStatus` called with `_if_false_subtree_ids`, winner loaded via `findStepById(_if_true_step_id)`, `queue.add` called with winner's action_type and data
Test: condition false → `updateManyStepsStatus` called with `_if_true_subtree_ids`, winner is `_if_false_step_id`
Test: `findStepById` returns null → `Error('Branch winner step not found: ...')` thrown
Test: `updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', ...)` is called BEFORE `evaluate` — verify call order
Test: `updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', ...)` is called AFTER `queue.add`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The `evaluate` import must be mocked — call `vi.mock('../../services/condition-evaluator.js', () => ({ evaluate: vi.fn() }))` then `import { evaluate } from '../../services/condition-evaluator.js'` after the mock. Cast `evaluate` to `vi.MockedFunction` for `mockReturnValue`. The branch step itself does NOT check for `_next_step_id` — branches are always terminal on their own; the continuation is encoded in the winning child's params. `exec_ctx` passed to `queue.add` is the same exec_ctx from the job (preserves event_id, execution_id, rule_id, rule_version across the whole chain).

userStories.title: enroll_sequence worker — field interpolation + POST /sequences/enroll
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an enroll_sequence action worker that resolves action params via field interpolation, strips routing metadata, and POSTs to the Nurturing Engine's /sequences/enroll endpoint — then enqueues the next step or marks the execution complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/services/action-workers/enroll-sequence.worker.ts`

Import `Job`, `Queue` from `bullmq`; import `type ActionJobData` from `../../queue/index.js`; import `type ExecutionRepository` from `../../repositories/execution.repository.js`; import `resolveParams`, `type ExecutionContext` from `../field-interpolator.js`
Export `const ENROLL_SEQUENCE_URL_ENV = 'NURTURING_ENGINE_URL'`

Export `createEnrollSequenceProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>, fetchFn?: typeof fetch): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`; `fetchFn` defaults to `globalThis.fetch`
The returned processor function:
1. Destructures `{ execution_id, step_id, action_params, exec_ctx, event }` from `job.data`
2. Calls `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`
3. Resolves: `const resolved = resolveParams(action_params, event, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`
4. Extracts `_next_step_id` from resolved: `const nextStepId = resolved['_next_step_id'] as string | undefined`
5. Strips ALL routing keys from the HTTP body: `const { _next_step_id: _, _if_true_step_id: _a, _if_false_step_id: _b, _if_true_subtree_ids: _c, _if_false_subtree_ids: _d, ...callParams } = resolved`
6. Gets base URL: `const baseUrl = process.env[ENROLL_SEQUENCE_URL_ENV]`. If falsy, throw `new Error('NURTURING_ENGINE_URL is not configured')`
7. Calls `const response = await fetchFn(baseUrl + '/sequences/enroll', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify(callParams) })`
8. If `!response.ok`, throw `new Error('enroll_sequence HTTP error: ' + response.status)`
9. Calls `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date() })`

10. If `nextStepId`: load `const nextStep = await repo.findStepById(nextStepId)`. If not null, call `await queue.add(nextStep.action_type, { execution_id, step_id: nextStepId, action_type: nextStep.action_type, action_params: nextStep.action_params as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx, event })`
11. If no `nextStepId`: call `await repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', new Date())`
`test/unit/action-workers/enroll-sequence-worker.test.ts` mocks `ExecutionRepository`, `Queue`, and passes a mock `fetchFn`; covers:

Test: happy path WITH `_next_step_id` — fetch called with correct URL and stripped body, step completed, `repo.findStepById(nextStepId)` called, `queue.add` called with next step data; `repo.updateExecutionStatus` NOT called
Test: happy path WITHOUT `_next_step_id` — fetch called, step completed, `repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', ...)` called; `queue.add` NOT called
Test: HTTP error (mock `fetchFn` returns `{ ok: false, status: 503 }`) — throws Error containing '503'
Test: missing env var (unset `process.env.NURTURING_ENGINE_URL`) — throws Error containing 'NURTURING_ENGINE_URL'
Test: routing keys stripped from POST body — action_params include `_next_step_id` and `_if_true_step_id`; `JSON.parse(fetchFn.calls[0][1].body)` has neither key
Test: field interpolation applied — action_params has `entity_id: 'payload.entity_id'`; event has `payload: { entity_id: 'lead-123' }`; POST body has `entity_id: 'lead-123'`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use `process.env[ENROLL_SEQUENCE_URL_ENV]` (bracket notation with the exported constant) — not a hardcoded string — so tests can set `process.env.NURTURING_ENGINE_URL`. Remember to `delete process.env.NURTURING_ENGINE_URL` in the missing-env-var test (or use `vi.stubEnv`). The `fetchFn` injection makes HTTP mocking trivial in tests without patching globalThis. `resolveParams` already handles nested objects recursively — no need to recurse manually.

userStories.title: emit_event worker + index.ts wiring — EventBridge publish and full Phase 4 assembly
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an emit_event action worker that publishes resolved payloads to EventBridge, plus full wiring in index.ts that replaces the ExecutionManager stub with the real implementation and starts all Phase 4 workers — so the pipeline is end-to-end functional.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `@aws-sdk/client-eventbridge` to `dependencies` in `package.json` and run `npm install`
Create `src/services/action-workers/emit-event.worker.ts`

Import `Job`, `Queue` from `bullmq`; import `type ActionJobData` from `../../queue/index.js`; import `type ExecutionRepository` from `../../repositories/execution.repository.js`; import `resolveParams`, `type ExecutionContext` from `../field-interpolator.js`; import `EventBridgeClient`, `PutEventsCommand` from `@aws-sdk/client-eventbridge`
Export `const EVENTBRIDGE_BUS_ENV = 'EVENTBRIDGE_BUS_NAME'`

Export `createEmitEventProcessor(repo: ExecutionRepository, queue: Queue<ActionJobData>, ebClient?: EventBridgeClient): (job: Job<ActionJobData>) => Promise<void>`; `ebClient` defaults to `new EventBridgeClient({})`
The returned processor function:
1. Destructures `{ execution_id, step_id, action_params, exec_ctx, event }` from `job.data`
2. Calls `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'running', { attempt: job.attemptsMade + 1 })`
3. Gets bus name: `const busName = process.env[EVENTBRIDGE_BUS_ENV]`. If falsy, throw `new Error('EVENTBRIDGE_BUS_NAME is not configured')`
4. Gets event_type: `const eventType = action_params['event_type'] as string`
5. Resolves payload: `const rawPayload = action_params['payload'] as Record<string, unknown> ?? {}`. Call `const resolvedPayload = resolveParams(rawPayload, event, exec_ctx as ExecutionContext)`
6. Calls `await ebClient.send(new PutEventsCommand({ Entries: [{ EventBusName: busName, Source: 'automation-engine', DetailType: eventType, Detail: JSON.stringify(resolvedPayload) }] }))`
7. Calls `await repo.updateStepStatus(step_id, 'completed', { completedAt: new Date() })`
8. Reads `_next_step_id = action_params['_next_step_id'] as string | undefined`

9. If `_next_step_id`: load `const nextStep = await repo.findStepById(_next_step_id)`. If not null, call `await queue.add(nextStep.action_type, { execution_id, step_id: _next_step_id, action_type: nextStep.action_type, action_params: nextStep.action_params as Record<string, unknown>, exec_ctx, event })`
10. If no `_next_step_id`: call `await repo.updateExecutionStatus(execution_id, 'completed', new Date())`
`test/unit/action-workers/emit-event-worker.test.ts` mocks `ExecutionRepository`, `Queue`, and passes a mock `ebClient` with `send: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})`; covers:

Test: `ebClient.send` called with `PutEventsCommand`; verify the Entries[0] has correct `EventBusName`, `Source: 'automation-engine'`, `DetailType = action_params.event_type`, `Detail = JSON.stringify(resolvedPayload)`
Test: payload field interpolation — `action_params.payload.entity_id = 'payload.entity_id'`; event has `payload: { entity_id: 'lead-abc' }`; resolved Detail contains `entity_id: 'lead-abc'`
Test: missing EVENTBRIDGE_BUS_NAME env var → throws Error

userStories.notes: For the `ebClient.send` test: `PutEventsCommand` is a class — mock `@aws-sdk/client-eventbridge` with `vi.mock('@aws-sdk/client-eventbridge', () => ({ EventBridgeClient: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({ send: vi.fn() })), PutEventsCommand: vi.fn().mockImplementation((input) => input) }))`. The `Detail` field on the EventBridge entry is a JSON string (not an object) — always `JSON.stringify(resolvedPayload)`. In `index.ts`, remove the now-unused `executionManagerStub` object literal and `ExecutionManagerPort` type import (the import comes from `event-consumer.ts` which still exports it — just remove the usage of the stub). Do NOT remove the `ExecutionManagerPort` export from `event-consumer.ts`.
